stories.headline: Zelenskyy asks Trump to send missiles after Russian strikes across Ukraine
stories.summary: At least 18 killed, dozens injured and others trapped under collapsed buildings after attacks on five Ukrainian citiesVolodymyr Zelenskyy has asked Donald Trump to send Patriot missiles to Ukraine after a devastating Russian attack killed at least 18 people and injured dozens more.Russia launched 73 missiles and 656 drones at Ukraine overnight, according to the air force, including eight hypersonic Tsirkon missiles. stories.headline: ‘Trapped’: Gaza patients flown to Iraq stuck in administrative limbo
stories.summary: Palestinian patients stripped of their documents are confined inside a Baghdad medical centre.

stories.headline: Ukraine rescuers pull dead from rubble after Russian strikes kill 21 people
stories.summary: An eight-year-old boy and a woman killed in a strike on an apartment block are among the dead, officials say.

stories.headline: Senegal Bonds Go From Emerging-Market Leaders to Laggards as Default Fears Rise
stories.summary: Senegal’s dollar bonds traded near record lows Tuesday as a deepening political crisis in the West African nation turned a top emerging-markets performer into the worst.

stories.headline: How the war has made Iran’s water crisis worse
stories.summary: Attacks on desalination plants and other water infrastructure by US and Israel have further added to the water woes.

stories.headline: Clashes continue in Lebanon despite Israel and Hezbollah accepting US partial ceasefire plan
stories.summary: A cessation of hostilities between Israel and Hezbollah is seen as crucial to the peace process with Iran.

stories.headline: Denmark's Mette Frederiksen to form government after months of negotiations
stories.summary: The leader of Denmark's Social Democratic Party is forming a centre-left coalition minority government, giving her a third term as prime minister.

stories.headline: Trump appoints ally as acting director of US national intelligence
stories.summary: Housing industry executive Bill Pulte has no known experience in national security

stories.headline: US in talks to expand nuclear capable deployments in Europe
stories.summary: Washington has signalled openness to additional countries hosting nuclear-capable bombers

stories.headline: Trump Names Bill Pulte as Acting Director of National Intelligence
stories.summary: Bill Pulte, a Trump loyalist who leads the federal housing finance agency, will have oversight of the United States intelligence agencies. He has no known background in intelligence.

stories.headline: Rubio to Testify Before Senate, Arguing for Budget Amid Iran War and Pressure on Cuba
stories.summary: The secretary of state has played a key role in the Iran deliberations, as well as in U.S. policy toward Cuba.

stories.headline: Trump Backs Off Plan for $1.8 Billion Fund That Drew Political Backlash
stories.summary: The fund has drawn backlash from critics who said it was a scheme to reward President Trump’s political allies with public benefits.

stories.headline: Mozambique says five citizens killed in ‘xenophobic attacks’ in South Africa
stories.summary: South African police confirm two deaths of Mozambicans in Mossel Bay as anti-immigration violence sweeps countryMozambique said five of its nationals were killed in “xenophobic attacks” in South Africa at the weekend and efforts were under way on Tuesday to repatriate hundreds of others.However, the South African police confirmed only that two Mozambicans had died in violence in the southern coastal town of Mossel Bay, the first killings to be of

stories.headline: Denmark’s Mette Frederiksen to lead four-party coalition after months of uncertainty
stories.summary: Social Democrat stays on for third consecutive term as PM and will outline key policies by the end of TuesdayDenmark’s new government is preparing to formally present its political programme after Mette Frederiksen negotiated a third consecutive term as prime minister, this time at the head of a four-party, left-leaning minority coalition.Announced late on Monday, the agreement between Frederiksen’s Social Democrats, the Social Liberals, the Gree

stories.headline: World Cup delay for Switzerland’s Embolo while US reviews travel document
stories.summary: Embolo is Switzerland's first-choice forward, with 24 goals in 86 internationals, but could not join the squad.

stories.headline: Two people shot dead amid Kenya protests against US Ebola quarantine centre plan
stories.summary: The US plan has sparked public anger and led to demonstrations near the site of the proposed treatment facility.

stories.headline: Ghana's anti-LGBTQ+ bill to be scrutinised before approval, president says
stories.summary: Passed by MPs, the bill proposes prison sentences for people identifying as part of the LGBTQ+ community.

stories.headline: South Africa police investigate killing of two Mozambican men
stories.summary: The deaths come after a day of violent protests against illegal migration in the town of Mossel Bay.

stories.headline: Australian ex-minister launches crowd-funded inquiry into Aukus submarine deal
stories.summary: Inquiry will look at whether Australia will receive the submarines and whether it will make the country safer.

stories.headline: China goes after 'ghost kitchens' to rein in cut-throat food delivery apps
stories.summary: The thousands of "ghost kitchens" - online shops that don't actually exist - have spooked Chinese consumers.

stories.headline: Why Myanmar president's India visit is being closely watched
stories.summary: Myanmar is seeking to broaden diplomatic engagement after years of international criticism and isolation.

stories.headline: How Deutsche Bank learned to stop chasing America
stories.summary: After decades of upheaval, the lender that wanted to take on Wall Street has scaled back its ambitions to focus on Europe

stories.headline: How did Spain’s unemployment rate converge with Finland’s?
stories.summary: Migration policy and labour market reforms are affecting joblessness — but in very different ways

stories.headline: US convertible bonds set for record year as issuers harness AI boom
stories.summary: Investors embrace zero-interest debt for options on high-growth tech stocks

stories.headline: Israel’s Netanyahu faces backlash after Trump call
stories.summary: Prime minister failed to follow through on threat to bomb Hizbollah after pressure from US president

stories.headline: Gold replaces US Treasuries as world’s top reserve asset, ECB says
stories.summary: Historic bullion rally boosts metal’s share of reserves to 27% while central banks diversify away from dollar

stories.headline: SpaceX Seeks IPO Fee Cut From Wall Street Bankers
stories.summary: SpaceX is negotiating to pay razor-thin fees to Wall Street firms handling its IPO, with banks still likely to rake in about $500 million from the record-setting debut. Sridhar Natarajan reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UniCredit Says Commerzbank Offer to Push Direct Stake to 34%
stories.summary: UniCredit SpA’s takeover offer for Commerzbank AG has been accepted by enough investors to give it a 34% stake once it takes ownership of the shares, adding momentum to its long running attempt to buy its German rival.

stories.headline: Large Prepaid Energy Deals Poised to Hit Booming Muni Sector
stories.summary: The muni market is slated to see a prepaid electricity deal amounting to as much as $1.25 billion that will add to the onslaught of supply in the surging market segment.

stories.headline: Triton Is Said to Near €3 Billion Deal for Carlyle’s Flender
stories.summary: Triton Partners is nearing a deal to buy Flender GmbH, a provider of mechanical and electrical drive technology, in a transaction valuing the firm at about €3 billion ($3.5 billion), according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: US Natural Gas Futures Fall on Lower Flows to LNG Export Plants
stories.summary: US natural gas futures declined as domestic gas flows to liquefied natural gas export terminals along the US Gulf Coast dropped for a third consecutive day. The reduction is concentrated at Sabine Pass LNG in Louisiana and Corpus Christi LNG in Texas.

stories.headline: Marvell Soars After Nvidia Chief’s $1 Trillion Stock Call
stories.summary: Marvell Technology Inc. shares surged after Nvidia Corp.’s Jensen Huang predicted the semiconductor and networking company will be the next business to hit a $1 trillion valuation, more than five times its current market capitalization.

stories.headline: Barclays Pushes UK to Be More Welcoming to Foreign Investment
stories.summary: Barclays Plc urged the UK to do more to attract a broader range of foreign investment and help add trillions of pounds more capital from overseas.

stories.headline: Musket balls and a burnt hull: evidence of real pirates of the Caribbean found in Bahamas

stories.headline: Massive fire engulfs Jakarta market leaving hundreds homeless
stories.summary: Hundreds of people have been made homeless after a huge fire tore through a market in Jakarta.

stories.summary: Analysts told BBC Verify that Tehran's strikes were more extensive than publicly acknowledged.

stories.headline: Stock Rally Wavers as Traders Weigh Peace Outlook: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: A rally that drove stocks to a series of all-time highs wavered as Wall Street traders weighed prospects for an agreement that would end the war that has roiled financial markets around the world.

stories.summary: Community groups say some fear they could lose homes, jobs and access to healthcare if the new law is ratified by President John Dramani MahamaGhana’s LGBTQ community is living in fear after the country’s parliament approved a sweeping bill that criminalises the promotion of LGBTQ+ activities and identifying as lesbian, gay, bisexual, transgender or queer, rights groups have warned.The legislation, which was passed on Friday, mandates prison sent

stories.headline: Typhoon Jangmi threatens Japan as Europe swelters
stories.summary: Powerful winds and rain expected in parts of Japan and Australia, while temperatures in Spain could hit 40CA powerful tropical storm is forecast to track near Okinawa, Japan, on Monday before moving towards the south-east of the country. Typhoon Jangmi (also known as Typhoon No 6) has formed within the monsoonal gyre over the Philippine Sea.A monsoonal gyre is a large, slow-rotating weather system that spawns typhoons through smaller vortices for

stories.headline: Trump administration will abide by ruling halting $1.8bn 'anti-weaponisation' fund
stories.summary: The justice department says it  "disagrees strongly" with a court decision to halt the establishment of the fund.

stories.headline: Hungarian PM threatens to oust Orbán-era president
stories.summary: Hungary's president has refused Prime Minister Péter Magyar's demands to step aside, setting up a constitutional clash.

stories.headline: Watch: Glass doors break as fans scramble to see Pursuit of Jade star
stories.summary: Huge crowds formed outside a shopping centre in China to see actor Zhang Linghe.

stories.headline: Huge blast kills dozens in rebel-held village in Myanmar
stories.summary: Insurgents say it was caused by explosives being used for mining close to the Chinese border.

stories.headline: Oil Swings as Traders Weigh Prospects of US-Iran Peace Deal
stories.summary: Oil flipped between gains and losses as traders parsed discordant signals on the prospects of a US-Iran peace deal.

stories.headline: Japan defence minister denies militarism and criticises China's 'huge arsenal'
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi makes some of Tokyo's most pointed remarks yet in the dispute over Japan's military build-up.

stories.headline: Secret tunnels and unregistered workers: China's coal mine disaster is a reminder of darker days
stories.summary: China's worst coal mining disaster in 15 years comes amid an ambitious pivot towards green energy.

stories.headline: Caribbean hot sauce producers warn of shortages and higher prices
stories.summary: Manufacturers in Jamaica say the key chilli peppers they need are in limited supply.

stories.headline: WHO calls for community cooperation to contain Ebola outbreak in DRC
stories.summary: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus makes appeal after protests against protocols for handling victims’ bodies in Ituri provinceContaining the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo requires community cooperation and is “everybody’s business”, the World Health Organization has said.Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, the organisation’s director general, made the plea on Sunday during a visit to eastern Congo where some residents have protested against

stories.headline: Japan defence minister rebuffs claims of ‘new militarism’ levelled by China
stories.summary: Shinjiro Koizumi says Japan valued as a ‘peace-loving’ nation while China expands military capabilities ‘without sufficient transparency’Japan’s defence minister took a veiled swipe at China on Sunday, pledging to keep strengthening the military despite Beijing’s criticism of Tokyo’s increasingly muscular security stance.Under the prime minister, Sanae Takaichi, Japan has quickened its pivot to a more proactive defence policy, further shaking off

stories.headline: How Putin became master of the image
stories.summary: From enigmatic KGB agent to wartime ruler, this is how Putin has repeatedly reinvented his image, and himself.

stories.headline: Ecuador accused of meddling in Colombian election with tariff vow
stories.summary: Ecuadorian leader Daniel Noboa offers to cancel tariffs after speaking to a right-wing presidential candidate ahead of Sunday's vote.

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.headline: US and Iran 'very close' to deal but 'not there yet', Vance says
stories.summary: US officials earlier told the BBC that the framework of a ceasefire extension deal had been agreed, pending the approval of Trump and Iran's leadership.

stories.headline: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
stories.summary: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: Learning from Ukraine war, Hezbollah is now using fibre-optic drones to hit Israel
stories.summary: Fibre-optic drones are now Hezbollah's primary weapon against Israel's soldiers and civilians.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
stories.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

stories.headline: Bolivian Congress allows deployment of troops to quell protests
stories.summary: Lawmakers who back the measure say it is key to restoring normality after four weeks of road blocks.

stories.headline: Can EU find a Russia whisperer to mediate an end to war in Ukraine?
stories.summary: With the US pulling out of trilateral talks with Russia and Ukraine, the EU is looking for potential candidates to step in.
